~/notes/ to the agent home directory (~/).  The notes repo is now used
only for memory, reports, handoffs, and long-term knowledge.

- tests: update hash-regression and ping-pong tests for new paths

commit.message:
Fix Playwright CLI provenance verification for tag format change (#16087)

* Fix Playwright CLI provenance verification for tag format change

@playwright/cli 0.1.7 changed their git tag naming convention from
'v0.1.7' to '0.1.7' (dropped the 'v' prefix). This caused our
provenance verification to fail with WorkflowRefMismatch.

- Accept both tag formats (with and without 'v' prefix) in the
  validateWorkflowRef callback for forward/backward compatibility
- Add test verifying both tag formats pass validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add E2E test for aspire new with agent init and Playwright install

Exercises the full aspire new → agent init → Playwright CLI install
flow end-to-end, verifying no provenance verification errors appear.
This is the primary regression test for tag format changes in upstream
@playwright/cli releases.

The test accepts the agent init prompt (instead of declining), selects
Playwright CLI during skill selection, and fails fast if a provenance
verification error is detected during npm package installation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: model version management — admin assigns versions, Jetson auto-builds engines

Dashboard (DevicesPage):
- Fetch active model versions per leaf_type from model_registry
- Model version selector per leaf_type in device edit form
- model_versions included in desired_config on save
- Display model versions + engine status (ready/building/error) in device table
- Config sync check ignores Jetson-added fields (engine_status, applied_model_versions)

Jetson (sync_engine):
- Detect model_versions change in polled desired_config
- Auto-build: check engine cache → download ONNX → trtexec --fp16 → hot-swap
- Exact version ONNX download (REST query by leaf_type+version with RPC fallback)
- Upload built engines to Storage cache for reuse by other devices
- Report engine_status + applied_model_versions in reported_config
- Re-report config on build completion/failure so dashboard updates
- Thread-safe config persistence (_config_lock protects read-modify-write)
- get_model_version() API for correct version tracking in predictions

Jetson (inference):
- InferenceWorkerPool.hot_swap() — queue-based swap on CUDA worker thread
- _do_hot_swap: load new engine BEFORE destroying old (graceful degradation)
- 120s timeout for slow Jetson devices

Docs:
- jetson_deployment_guide: section 9.5 Model Version Management
- admin_dashboard_manual: updated Device Fleet Tab
- project_context: model version management architecture

commit.message:
Task 25: Award XP on enemy kill

Award 1 XP per enemy kill in main.cpp's dead-enemy detection loop.
When an enemy is first detected as dead (not yet in the respawn queue),
playerState.awardXP(1) is called before queuing the respawn. With
XP_TO_LEVEL=5, the player levels up every 5 kills. Respawned enemies
award XP again when re-killed.

ensuring the scrollbar fits exactly within the PDF view container
without extending under the mode line.

- Drag state stored on PdfState entity (survives across frames)

commit.message:
fix remaining video seek clicks: mute before DSP + race-free ordering

Two fixes for clicks that only affected video (stream-from-disk) path:

1. Race condition: setNextReadPosition changed the source position BEFORE
   setting the mute flag — audio thread could read new-position data at full
   volume in the gap. Now sets mute flag FIRST.

2. Mute/fade was applied AFTER EQ/inserts — IIR filters processed the raw
   discontinuity and generated transients even though output got zeroed.
   Moved mute/fade BEFORE DSP so filters see silence during the mute window
   and a gentle ramp during fade-in. No explicit filter reset needed — they
   naturally decay to zero processing silence.
